pattern: Assigning each character to a row that bounces between top and bottom turns the visual zigzag into a simple per-row bucket concatenation.
hints: You do not need to build the 2D picture - you only need to know which row each character belongs to as you read the string.
Walk down the rows then back up, reversing direction at the top and bottom; collect characters into one bucket per row.

Keep a list of strings, one per row. Move a row index down by one each step, flip direction at row 0 and row numRows-1, append each character to its row, then join all rows. Guard numRows == 1 (no zigzag).

edge_inputs.description: single row leaves the string unchanged
numRows larger than the string length
two rows split into evens and odds
four rows with diagonal characters between verticals

stress.description: random string with a random row count up to the length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ') for _ in range(n))
    numRows = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [s, numRows]
stress.note: row count bounded by length so it stays in range
